data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration of fe57's premises (the foundation story's fixtures+fake are grounded in real surfaces, not fictional):
- All four enrichment methods fe57 fakes resolve on the real client with the exact signatures referenced: get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391), get_parent_map (L189), search_issues (acli.py L370). The fake returning these from "the real method signatures" is accurate.
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot (fetcher.py L480) merges the enrichment maps into snapshot entries through the production path exactly as fe57 describes: comment field merged as the nested raw Jira dict {"comments":[...]} (L413-417; get_comment_map docstring L303-308 confirms the raw nested shape — the bug-0ee6 seam), issuelinks list merged (L450-454; bug-3f04 enrichment), parent map merged. So "the fake adds NO shape massaging; fixtures flow through production fetch_snapshot enrichment" is realizable against current code.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's file_impact (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json, tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py, scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py + re-capture doc) is net-new; no sibling co-edits these. 59ef adds test_acliclient_contract.py (and only READS acli_graph.py), 8937 adds _snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py + fetcher.py producer-validates, 822a adds test_roundtrip.py + edits the probe script, 33d0/2359 edit the outbound applier cluster. fe57 only READS the production fetcher/enrichment surface through the fake; no shared mutable file state.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937's snapshot-entry schema (the unmassaged fixtures are precisely the shapes the schema targets), 59ef AC2 (an unmassaged fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes the shape contract), and 822a's behavioral round-trip. No representation contradiction. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry mismatch tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by fe57's inbound-enrichment fixtures/fake.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the declared FOUNDATION ("the foundation every other story builds on"); the three consumer siblings each declare depends_on fe57 (8937, 59ef, 822a). fe57 itself correctly depends on nothing — it is the root producer of fixtures+fake. It scopes the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef) explicitly OUT, consistent with the roster. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns the fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script; 59ef explicitly scopes "capturing the fixtures (story A)" OUT and consumes the fake; 8937 explicitly scopes "capturing fixtures (story A)" OUT and consumes them; 822a uses "story A's fixtures/fake" and scopes the schema OUT. Clean producer/consumer split — fe57 PRODUCES the artifact each sibling CONSUMES; no duplicated deliverable.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The hard ordering edge (fixtures+fake must exist before any consuming test) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57's files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that hazard.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57 is purely additive (new fixtures, fake, capture script, doc). Its AC4 ("consumed only via the production code path; no test reads them and hand-reshapes") and the documented re-capture cadence ("so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") align with parent AC7 ("no net increase in brittle tests") and the epic's anti-change-detector posture. It imposes no brittle expectation on any consumer sibling.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four faked enrichment method signatures exist on the real client (verified above), and the production fetch_snapshot merge points are live. 33d0 deletes the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface fe57 fakes, so its deletion strands nothing fe57 references. The secret-scrubbing/capture script targets live Jira behind a gate (no committed-secret hazard for siblings).

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (a documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 COVERAGE matter already tracked across the sibling set (33d0/2359/59ef/822a/8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here. (Note: fe57's re-capture doc "so fixtures do not rot into change-detectors" is self-scoped to its own fixtures, not the governing artifact AC6 requires — consistent with the G3 finding, not a new G4 issue.)

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
